summary: This brief post announces a Bloomberg View article about Facebook's potential to measure the impact of Russian ads on the 2016 election. O'Neil argues that Facebook has the data and capability to determine whether election meddling made a difference, but questions whether the company has the willingness to conduct such analysis. The post serves primarily as a pointer to the fuller argument in the Bloomberg piece.
key_points: Facebook possesses the data to measure Russian ad impact on the 2016 election
The company could conduct a natural experiment but chooses not to
Corporate willingness, not technical capability, is the barrier to transparency
money_quotes: The company could probably measure the effect of election-meddling if it wanted to.
key_insight: Facebook has the power to answer crucial questions about election interference but lacks the motivation to pursue uncomfortable truths.
